summary: Recorded the core search/RAG architecture decision (pgvector + tsvector + RRF, OpenAI embeddings, Vercel AI SDK on SvelteKit 2/adapter-node), three build gotchas as learnings (postgres-js db.execute returns rows directly; AI SDK v4 streamText exposes async textStream; $env/dynamic/private breaks under plain tsx → use process.env), and 4 new pages (RRF concept, /api/search + /api/ask entities, embed-bookmarks script entity). Also fixed E1 (file-tree.md missing frontmatter) and regenerated the file tree to reflect the new SvelteKit project structure.

user: Search your bookmarks by meaning, or ask natural-language questions with cited answers
what: Shipped a new SvelteKit search app: a search UI backed by hybrid semantic+keyword ranking, plus an "Ask" mode that streams RAG answers grounded in your bookmarks with citations.
why: Lets you find and query 2,270 bookmarks by intent rather than exact text match. Live search goes active once you set OPENAI_API_KEY and run the embedding backfill.
next: Add an OpenAI API key to .env, run `npm run embed`, then `npm run dev` to try search and Ask mode.

user: Search works again — no more error when the page loads
what: The /api/search endpoint was returning 500 because the database connection string was missing inside the dev server. Fixed by loading dotenv in the shared db module.
why: Search is the core feature; a 500 on load made the whole app appear broken.
next: Reload the app — search returns results in under a second.

user: The app's new visual direction is now documented with exact colors, fonts, and signature elements ready to implement.
what: Documented the agentos-sdk.dev design system restyle decision, a key gotcha, and the extracted token reference.
why: Future sessions need the exact design tokens and the rationale to keep the restyle faithful and consistent.
next: Apply the restyle to app.css and components per the decision; grep /tmp/agentos.css for exact rules.
summary: Recorded the decision to adopt the agentos-sdk.dev visual design system (warm-paper light theme, chrome-gradient borders, Manrope/JetBrains Mono, exact color tokens), a learning flagging the unused shadcn dark HSL vars trap, and an artifact page capturing the full extracted design system with source file references.

user: The search UI now wears the agentos-sdk.dev design language — warm paper background, hairline borders, and a signature animated chrome border on the search input.
what: Restyled the entire search interface (app.css, app.html, +page.svelte) to match agentos-sdk.dev: Manrope + JetBrains Mono fonts, fractal-noise paper grain, warm paper/cream/ink palette with primary-orange CTAs, font-medium headlines with tight tracking, hairline-bordered result cards with hover lift, and the signature animated silver conic-gradient chrome border on the focal search input.
why: Gives the app a distinctive, premium visual identity instead of a generic look, matching the agentos aesthetic the team chose to adopt.
next: View the search page to see the new design; ask to extend the treatment to mobile/focus states or adjust orange intensity / grain.

user: Buttons, links, and other interactive controls now show a pointer cursor on hover, so it's obvious what's clickable.
what: Added `cursor: pointer` to `button`, `a`, `[role='button']`, submit/button/reset/checkbox/radio inputs, `summary`, and `label` in src/app.css. Text inputs keep their text cursor.
why: Provides standard hover affordance — users can immediately tell which elements are interactive without clicking to find out.
next: Hover any button or link to see the pointer. Want the whole result card to feel clickable? Ask to lift the inner `<a>` to wrap the card.

user: Documented the switch to Voyage 1024-dim embeddings and the free-tier rate-limit gotcha
what: Recorded a decision (Voyage voyage-4-large @ 1024-dim, supersedes the OpenAI embedding clause) and a learning (Voyage 3 RPM / 10K TPM cap without a payment method).
why: Future sessions need to know the corpus is now 1024-dim Voyage vectors and why a full re-embed was required.
summary: Captured the embedding-provider pivot (OpenAI 384-dim → Voyage voyage-4-large 1024-dim) as a decision superseding the prior stack decision's embedding clause, and recorded the Voyage free-tier 3 RPM/10K TPM rate-limit gotcha as a learning. Both reference the 1024-dim migration, rewritten embeddings.ts (input_type query/document + 429 backoff), and the blocked backfill.

Recorded a reusable rule: sanitize text (strip lone surrogates + control chars) at the embedding API boundary, after a Voyage HTTP 400 killed the backfill mid-job. Also fixed a broken wikilink in the Voyage embedding decision pointing to the rate-limit learning.

Recorded the reranking architecture extension and supporting knowledge from the rerank + content-cap + daily-workflow turn: a decision adopting Voyage rerank-2.5-lite cross-encoder reranking on top of RRF hybrid search (pool of 50 → top limit, with fallback); a rule for the pool-then-rerank-with-graceful-fallback retrieval pattern; a learning that the Postgres FTS "word too long" NOTICE for >2047-char tokens is harmless; and two new entity pages — the rerank service (src/lib/server/rerank.ts) and the daily embed-new-bookmarks GitHub Actions workflow.

Recorded a learning: GitHub requires the `workflow` scope on the push token for any commit touching `.github/workflows/`, even when the token has `repo`. Documents the creatuluw token push rejection, the split-commit workaround already used, and the one-time `gh auth refresh -s workflow` fix or GitHub UI route.

Recorded two learnings from a Railway/Railpack deploy-debugging turn: (1) adapter-node requires the ORIGIN env var set to the public URL or POST/form requests fail with 403 CSRF errors, and (2) Railpack requires a start command (a "start" script, "main" field, or root index.js) — for SvelteKit + adapter-node the fix is adding `"start": "node build"` to package.json scripts.

user: The wiki overview and file-tree now reflect the actual productsear.ch app instead of the empty scaffold description
what: Overview rewritten to describe the real semantic bookmark search app (SvelteKit + pgvector + Voyage embeddings/rerank + DeepSeek RAG); file-tree updated for the Voyage migration, new rerank module, new SQL migrations, GitHub embed workflow, and static assets
why: Overview still claimed "no application source code exists" and file-tree listed OpenAI embeddings; both were stale after the d8e6efa→3dbaf21 commits
summary: Rewrote docs/wiki/overview.md as a real system overview (tech stack, layout, knowledge wiki). Updated docs/wiki/architecture/file-tree.md: embeddings.ts now Voyage voyage-4-large 1024-dim, added rerank.ts, migration-384.sql + migration-voyage-1024.sql, moved research_report.md under docs/, added .github/workflows/embed-new-bookmarks.yml and static/favicon.svg, noted package.json `start` script. Decisions/learnings for these changes were already captured by the recap subagent.
